quiz_questions.question_text: What is the SI unit of force?
quiz_questions.explanation: SI units: Force = Newton (N = kg·m/s²), Energy/Work = Joule (J = kg·m²/s²), Power = Watt (W = J/s), Momentum = kg·m/s. 1 horsepower ≈ 746 W. The answer is 'Newton (N)'. The other options — 'Joule (J)', 'Watt (W)', and 'Pascal (Pa)' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

options.text: A diagram showing all forces on a single object as arrows
A diagram of an object in free fall
A graph of force vs. time
A picture of an object with no forces

quiz_questions.explanation: Free-Body Diagrams A free-body diagram shows all forces acting on a single object as arrows from its center of mass. The other options — 'A diagram of an object in free fall', 'A graph of force vs. time', and 'A picture of an object with no forces' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

options.text: The vector sum of all forces acting on an object
The largest force acting on an object
Any single force on the object

quiz_questions.explanation: A free body diagram shows all forces acting on an object. The net force (vector sum) determines acceleration via F_net = ma. If F_net = 0, the object is in equilibrium. The answer is 'The vector sum of all forces acting on an object'. The other options — 'The largest force acting on an object', 'The force of gravity', and 'Any single force on the object' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

quiz_questions.question_text: What happens when the net force on an object is zero?

options.text: The object is in equilibrium — no acceleration
The object must be at rest

quiz_questions.explanation: A free body diagram shows all forces acting on an object. The net force (vector sum) determines acceleration via F_net = ma. If F_net = 0, the object is in equilibrium. The answer is 'The object is in equilibrium — no acceleration'. The other options — 'The object must be at rest', 'The object must be moving', and 'The object accelerates' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

quiz_questions.question_text: Is force a scalar or vector?
quiz_questions.explanation: Vectors have both magnitude and direction (velocity, force, acceleration). Scalars have only magnitude (speed, mass, energy). Vectors can be decomposed into perpendicular components. The answer is 'Vector'. The other options — 'Scalar', 'Both', and 'Neither' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

options.text: A force acting at a distance

quiz_questions.explanation: Types of Forces Contact forces : Require physical touch — friction, tension, normal force, applied force, air resistance. The other options — 'A force acting at a distance', 'A force from gravity', and 'An electromagnetic force' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.

quiz_questions.question_text: What is 1 Newton in base units?
quiz_questions.explanation: Derived units are combinations of base units. Newton (N) = kg·m/s² (force). Joule (J) = kg·m²/s² (energy). Watt (W) = J/s (power). Pascal (Pa) = N/m² (pressure). The answer is '1 kg·m/s²'. The other options — '1 kg·m/s', '1 kg·m²/s²', and '1 kg/m·s²' — are related concepts but do not correctly answer this specific question.
